crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Road InvITs AUM to grow 30% this fiscal led by asset monetisation surge
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Infrastructure investment trusts (InvITs) have emerged as a key investment vehicle for enabling monetisation of infrastructure assets by private as well as public sector entities. Specifically, road sector InvITs are poised to see their assets under management (AUM) rise to ~Rs 3.9 lakh crore by Mar

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: PVs steering towards record sales of 5.9 million units this fiscal
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: India’s passenger vehicle (PV) industry is set to see overall sales volume accelerate 5-7% to ~5.9 million units—its highest ever level—this fiscal, riding on the reduction in the goods and services tax (GST) last September and sustained consumer preference for utility vehicles.

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: HAM roads resilient despite execution delays in ~60% of the projects
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: A large portion of under-construction road projects (by length) under the hybrid annuity model (HAM) are trailing on execution schedule, yet their credit risk profiles remain stable, supported by protection under concession agreement.

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Flexible workspace capacity to grow 16-18% this fiscal and next
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Indian flexible workspace (flex) segment is set to increase its capacity by 16-18% over this and next fiscals to 140-145 million square feet (msf) - after an expansion with ~23% CAGR over the past three fiscals though fiscal 2026 - driven by rising demand from small and mid-sized global capability c

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: For banks, ECL norms to have one-time net impact of up to 120 bps
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: The Reserve Bank of India’s (RBI) final directions1 entailing, inter alia, transition to an expected credit loss (ECL) regime for domestic banks, would lead to a one-time net impact of up to 120 basis points (bps) on their Common Equity Tier 1 (CET-1) ratio.

crisil-research.title: Quickonomics: Price parts path-again
crisil-research.description: The dichotomy between inflation based on WPI and CPI is not new. CPI inflation has always been in the positive zone unlike WPI, which tends to swing between inflation and deflation, causing divergence between the two. Data for the past 15 fiscals shows divergence in 2015-16 and 2020 on account of th

crisil-research.title: Crisil Economy First Cut: Financial conditions continue to be tight
crisil-research.description: Financial conditions remained under pressure in April, with the Crisil Financial Conditions Index (FCI) standing at -1.2, a slight improvement from March’s -1.4. A negative FCI value, particularly one that falls outside the comfort band of one standard deviation, indicates financial conditions are s

crisil-research.title: Dual guardrails: PE support and redemption gates in BDCs
crisil-research.description: As the private credit market matures, business development companies (BDCs) have emerged as a vital bridge between institutional capital and middle-market companies managing over $500 billion assets. However, as retail participation in non-traded BDCs grows, so does the scrutiny regarding liquidity—

crisil-research.title: Indian Economy: Downside risks materialising
crisil-research.description: With the conflict in West Asia continuing for nearly two months, we see increasing downside risks to our base case gross domestic product (GDP) growth outlook for the current fiscal. If disruptions extend through April, we expect GDP growth to slow to 6.8% this fiscal, compared with our earlier proj

crisil-research.title: Global Economy: Clouds of conflict
crisil-research.description: Inflation based on the Consumer Price Index in the United States (US) surged to 3.3%, its highest reading since May 2024, driven by a 12.5% jump in energy inflation. The Federal Reserve maintained its policy rate at 3.5-3.75% in March.

crisil-research.title: Crisil Economy First Cut: Shielded by policy, so far
crisil-research.description: Inflation based on the Consumer Price Index (CPI) rose mildly to 3.48% in April from 3.40% in March. While the conflict in West Asia has crossed 74 days, the upside risks to retail inflation seem to be materialising at a snail’s pace, indicating that the consumer remains largely protected so far.

baroda-etrade-str.title: Oil & Gas: World Petrochemical Conference (WPC 2026): key takeaways
baroda-etrade-str.description: US-Iran conflict has disrupted petrochemical supply chains and also tightened the feedstock availability, leading to volatility in pricing

ssga-insights.title: Why Canada matters for developed ex-US equity allocation
ssga-insights.description: Investors using MSCI EAFE Index for their international allocation are missing out on exposure to Canada, the world’s ninth largest economy. Here’s why MSCI World ex USA may be a better option.
